legal.title: Legal, Privacy and Data Protection
legal.disclaimer: This page describes how Anglicus currently works and is not legal advice.
legal.overview: We update this policy when product behavior changes, so you can see what data is processed, where it is stored, and how to control it.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using Anglicus, you agree to these terms.
terms.point1: Anglicus is an educational platform. We do not guarantee specific learning outcomes, exam results, or uninterrupted availability.
terms.point2: You are responsible for account security, for content you submit, and for activity performed through your account.
terms.point3: You may not use the service for unlawful activity, abuse, automated scraping, security probing, or attempts to bypass limits.
terms.point4: AI-generated answers can be incorrect or incomplete. You should verify important information before relying on it.
terms.point5: Paid features and discounts follow the billing flow shown in the app. Crypto payments are irreversible once confirmed on-chain.
terms.point6: We may update, suspend, or remove features to improve reliability, safety, or legal compliance.
terms.outro: If you do not agree with these terms, you should stop using the service.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro: We minimize personal data and separate on-device learning data from limited server-side account data.
privacy.point1: On your device, we store profile, learning progress, settings, mistakes, and speaking statistics in local browser storage (localStorage and IndexedDB).
privacy.point2: For authentication, the API stores a user ID, hashed email, password hash (email login only), auth provider, and subscription status in our database.
privacy.point3: For product operations, we store usage counters and selected analytics events linked to your user ID.
privacy.point4: If you enable email reminders, we store email, reminder time, timezone offset, language, and frequency encrypted in reminder storage until you unsubscribe.
privacy.point5: If you use paid checkout, we store checkout session data such as address, network, required amount, transaction ID, confirmation state, and paid-until date.
privacy.point6: If you submit feedback, we process your message and optional contact email to respond and improve the product.
privacy.outro: We do not sell personal data and we do not use third-party advertising trackers.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.intro: Anglicus uses only essential cookies and application storage needed to run core features.
cookies.point1: The `anglicus_promo` cookie stores a signed promo token for billing discounts. It is HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, path-limited to `/api/billing`, and can last up to 1 year.
cookies.point2: The `anglicus_referral` cookie stores a signed referral token with the same security settings and lifetime.
cookies.point3: We do not set advertising, remarketing, or third-party analytics cookies.
cookies.point4: The app also uses localStorage and sessionStorage for auth tokens, language preference, settings, active checkout state, and anti-abuse controls.
cookies.point5: If you block or clear cookies/storage, some features (for example discounts or persistent login) may stop working until you sign in or apply codes again.
cookies.point6: Browser controls let you manage or delete cookies and local data at any time.
cookies.outro: Cookie values are signed tokens, not plain promo or referral codes.

dataProtection.title: Data Protection
dataProtection.intro: We apply technical and organizational controls appropriate for a small educational platform.
dataProtection.point1: Email identifiers are stored as hashes in auth records, and passwords are stored as bcrypt hashes.
dataProtection.point2: Reminder subscriptions are encrypted before being stored in reminder infrastructure.
dataProtection.point3: On-device profile storage uses encryption and integrity checks, with local backup logic to reduce tampering and accidental loss.
dataProtection.point4: To prevent abuse, rate-limiting systems may process your IP address or a proxy-derived network identifier.
dataProtection.point5: When required for service delivery, data can be sent to AI model providers, email delivery providers, payment or blockchain infrastructure, and Google Sign-In.
dataProtection.point6: Retention varies by dataset: reminder entries remain until unsubscribe, rate-limit records are short-lived, and account/billing/analytics records are kept while needed for operations, fraud prevention, and legal obligations.
dataProtection.outro: You can request access, correction, deletion, or reminder opt-out through Settings and in-app feedback. If local data remains, clear the site's browser storage for a full device-side wipe.
